factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin businesses and segments, particularly aerospace and technology, and industrial services. This shift is contributing to margin improvements and cost efficiency, with a strategic focus on higher profit businesses and lower margin field services playing a significant role in reducing costs relative to revenue. Overall, the changes in the revenue mix are positively impacting the company's profitability.

evidence.text: The reduction in cost relative to revenue is driven by 1) our strategic focus on realigning our portfolio to higher profit businesses (approximately two thirds of the reduction in costs relative to revenue) and 2) lower margin field services revenue as a percent of total revenue resulting in mix benefits (approximately one third of the reduction in costs relative to revenue).
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Aerospace and Technology – We provide an in-depth range of scientific, engineering, construction, nuclear and technical support services to the aerospace, defense, technical and automotive industries in several countries. [...] Industrial – We provide engineering, procurement, project management, construction, and on-site maintenance to our global clients in the Life Sciences, Mining & Minerals, Specialty Chemicals & Manufacturing and Field Services markets.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The shift towards higher-margin businesses is improving profitability.

factors.summary: The backlog is growing modestly, driven by new awards in key segments despite some project cancellations and currency impacts. The growth is slightly lower when considering foreign exchange effects, but still shows a positive trend overall.

evidence.text: Backlog increased $0.2 billion, or 1.4%, to $18.5 billion at March 31, 2017 from $18.2 billion at April 1, 2016. The $0.2 billion increase was due primarily to new awards from clients operating in the Buildings & Infrastructure LOB, U.S. federal government and the Chemicals industry partially offset by the cancellation of certain projects in the Life Sciences areas and an approximately $287.8 million reduction due to foreign exchange, and strong revenue associated with large pharma projects.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Backlog at March 31, 2017 was $18.5 billion, and represents an increase of $0.2 billion over backlog at April 1, 2016. Excluding the effects of changes in currency exchange rates, backlog was up $0.5 billion, or 2.9%, as compared to the corresponding period last year.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Backlog is growing despite some challenges, indicating positive demand trends.

factors.summary: Demand trends for Jacobs Engineering Group Inc. and its subsidiaries appear to be mixed. While the backlog has increased slightly and new orders have partially offset cancellations, there are signs of deceleration in certain segments such as Petroleum & Chemicals, Aerospace & Technology, and Industrial LOB’s. However, demand remains stable to growing in other sectors, supported by long-term contracts and consistent customer activity across various industries.

evidence.text: The $0.2 billion increase was due primarily to new awards from clients operating in the Buildings & Infrastructure LOB, U.S. federal government and the Chemicals industry partially offset by the cancellation of certain projects in the Life Sciences areas and an approximately $287.8 million reduction due to foreign exchange, and strong revenue associated with large pharma projects.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues was due primarily to lower volumes in the Petroleum & Chemicals, Aerospace & Technology and Industrial LOB’s, partially offset by an increase in volume in the Buildings & Infrastructure LOB. These lower volumes were driven mainly by lower field services volume, primarily with P&C customers and the timing of project completions versus new project timing.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: We have provided advanced technology engineering services to the DoD for more than 50 years, and currently support major defense programs in the U.S. and internationally. [...] We believe our global presence and understanding of contracting and delivery demands keep us well positioned to provide professional services worldwide.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand with some deceleration in certain segments and stability elsewhere.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory of Jacobs Engineering Group Inc. and its subsidiaries shows mixed results. evidence.text: The reduction in cost relative to revenue is driven by 1) our strategic focus on realigning our portfolio to higher profit businesses (approximately two thirds of the reduction in costs relative to revenue) and 2) lower margin field services revenue as a percent of total revenue resulting in mix benefits (approximately one third of the reduction in costs relative to revenue).

factors.summary: The company has implemented restructuring initiatives that are expected to deliver annual savings of approximately $285 million. Cost reduction actions from restructuring are delivering savings, on track with expectations. Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: The decrease in SG&A expenses for the three months ended March 31, 2017 as compared to the corresponding period last year was due primarily to higher savings associated with the 2015 Restructuring and other charges, mostly offset by higher year over year costs related to the 2015 Restructuring and other charges associated with our strategic business restructuring in our Europe, U.K. and Middle East regional operations.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Despite lower business volumes mentioned above, the increase in operating profit for the three months ended March 31, 2017 as compared to the prior year comparable period was primarily due to SG&A savings of $9.5 million from restructuring efforts and a $9.9 million one-time benefit associated with changes in benefit plans in our India operations.
evidence.subsection: Petroleum & Chemicals

evidence.text: The Company’s consolidated results of operations for the second fiscal quarters of 2017 and 2016 include $52.3 million and $35.4 million, respectively, and $84 million and $103.8 million for the six month periods ended March 31, 2017 and 2016, of pre-tax costs associated with the 2015 Restructuring. The 2015 Restructuring was substantially completed as of the end of the second fiscal quarter of 2017, with future expenses not expected to exceed $15 million. The Company expects annual savings from the 2015 Restructuring upon its completion to approximate $285 million per year.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has implemented successful restructuring initiatives that are delivering expected annual savings.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with some sectors experiencing growth while others are declining.
